justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Both queues identified by name (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) in component_interactions and new_queues_topics. (2) Both env vars identified by name (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME) in component_interactions and new_env_vars. (3) Escalations routed to Coach K with specific infrastructure items listed. (4) new_infrastructure_required section fully populated with new_queues_topics and new_env_vars. (5) Verdict is NOT READY. (6) Observability review acknowledges adequate logging baseline and health check while correctly identifying gaps in queue-level metrics — not a false positive.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough infrastructure gap identification — goes beyond minimum by listing provisioning steps per environment, network rules, and secrets manager entries
Correctly routes infrastructure escalations to Coach K, not directly to Shaq or MJ
Bonus escalation noting RabbitMQ was an unauthorized architectural decision routed to Coach K → MJ — shows strong spec compliance awareness
Observability review correctly distinguishes between what's adequate (structured logging, health check, p99 latency) and what's missing (queue depth, DLQ depth, consumer lag)
Resilience assessment identifies concrete failure modes (poison messages, OOM, duplicate delivery) beyond what the rubric requires

Some escalations route through Coach K to other agents (Shaq, MJ) which is acceptable but the rubric specifically wants Coach K as the target — the routing is correct since Coach K is the first recipient
new_infrastructure_required section is exceptionally detailed with config_changes and infra_provisioning sub-sections beyond minimum requirements
